preamble: The glottal stop, produced by a complete closure of the vocal cords, can undergo a phonetic transformation to a voiceless velar plosive, where the closure shifts to the back of the tongue against the soft palate. This change, often observed in specific dialectal contexts, represents a fortition, making the sound more prominent or aligning it with orthographic conventions.
sources: Huili Li, I. P. Baryadi, I. P. Wijana. (2019). SOUND PATTERN OF INDONESIAN PLOSIVES. Linguistik Indonesia.

Septhia Irnanda, Sabrina, Zaiyana Putri, Nurul Inayah, Nyak Mutia Ismail, Marisa Yoestara. (2021). The Plosive Sound Change in Indonesian Final -k Syllabic Words: A Case Study of Indonesian Acehnese Readers. Proceedings of the Thirteenth Conference on Applied Linguistics (CONAPLIN 2020). Atlantis Press.
